Force Snap  

Description

1: Decrease Hitpoints by 150. Max: 252
2: Stun (1.00 sec/55)

Details   Raw Spell Data, Lucy Spell View

Mana: 69 Skill: Evocation
Casting Time: 2.5 Recast Time: 13
Fizzle Time: 1.5 Resist: Magic
Range: 200 Location: Any
Time of Day: Any Push Back: 1
Fizzle Adj: 25 Deletable: Yes
Reflectable: Yes Focusable: Yes
Dispellable: Yes Interruptable: Yes
Target Type: Single Spell Type: Detrimental
Source: Live 12/03

Messages

Cast on you: You have been force struck.
Cast on other: Soandso has been force struck.

Game Description

Strikes your target with a burst of intense force, causing between 150 and @1 damage and stunning targets up to level @2 for 1 second.

Items with this effect

  • Spell: Force Snap

Quick Facts

Scroll:
Spell: Force Snap
ClassLevel
WIZ17
Expansion:

Duration:
Instant
  • Comments (16)
  • Sold By (6)
  • Quested (1)

Channeler Cerakoth
QuoteReply
# Sep 06 2005 at 10:19 AM Rating: Decent
PettiL
61 posts
This spell is only sold by Channeler Cerokth in PoK for 10p4g6s5c with an 80 cha, if that makes much of a difference anymore.
▲ Page top
Wizzy has Dagger Frost
QuoteReply
# Jun 05 2005 at 2:50 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591780143482
176 posts
Been using Dagger Frost since level 17 with ID1 focus. It has a long refresh rate, but works well with root and nuke. I have not seen any knockback. Two nukes will take down a paludal bandit if not resisted. I still use smaller nukes when ID1 adds a nice amount of damage. The spell levels were changed and this one is really really useful, still using at 24.

Edited, Sun Jun 5 03:54:04 2005

OK all Channeler Cerakoth does sell this spell but is not called Dagger of Frost,it is called Force Shock and it is a lvl 20 wizard spell

▲ Page top
Stun effect?
QuoteReply
# Oct 20 2003 at 9:45 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591780877105
12 posts
Since the stun rate is only 0.00, does that mean it doesn't actually stun the mob but does interrupt casting?

Love,

Wandriana Noamuth <Ancient Fellowship> Xev
(Necromancer of the 63th Season)
▲ Page top
Knoxkback Effect
QuoteReply
# Sep 07 2003 at 11:09 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1653869803792
Scholar
285 posts
This spell does have a knockback effect. It is a server side effect which means it is not in the spdat file so it doesn't show up in Lucy. That also means that when the spell hits, you won't see the mob knocked back until you get a refresh on the mobs location from the server, which often won't occur until several seconds after the spell lands.

This delay in knockback made it very difficult for our guild to determine the source of push after our tanks started loading up with the Great Blade of Storms.

--Roz

Edited, Sun Sep 7 12:07:25 2003
▲ Page top
RE: Knoxkback Effect
QuoteReply
# Apr 20 2004 at 12:43 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591772268732
5 posts
I am interested in your observation about server side effect, I am currently researchin knockback effects on all spells (particularly proc weapons) for the same reason (push in raids). Is this an assumption you have come up with to try and explain the push or has Sony stated something to this fact. Any help would be apreciated.


Soulfyst
▲ Page top
